Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log for 5 cases tickets 101 to 105 We need to identify cases with significantly longer total resolution times determine potential root causes of performance issues and explain how these factors lead to increased cycle times and propose recommendations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets first compute the total resolution time for each case from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Duration 2 hours 15 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Duration 25 hours 10 minutes or 1 day 1 hour 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Duration 1 hour 20 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Duration 24 hours 10 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Duration 49 hours 5 minutes about 2 days 1 hour 5 minutes

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So cases 102 104 and 105 are significantly longer than 101 and 103

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 101 fast No escalation smooth flow Case 103 fast No escalation smooth flow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 has an escalation at 2024 03 01 11 30 then Investigate Issue at 14 00 2 point 5 hours later then Resolve next day at 09 00 19 hours after investigation start Also from Assign to Level 1 Agent 09 00 to Escalate 11 30 is 2 point 5 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 has no escalation Activities Receive Triage Assign to Level 1 Investigate Issue at 13 00 which is 3 point 5 hours after assign Resolve next day 08 00 19 hours after investigate start So there is a 3 point 5 hour gap before investigation starts

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 has escalation Receive 08 25 Triage 08 35 Assign 09 00 Investigate 09 10 Escalate at 10 00 Then next Investigate Issue at 2024 03 02 14 00 28 hours after escalation Then Resolve at 2024 03 03 09 00 19 hours after second investigation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observations Escalations 102 and 105 seem to correlate with longer times but 104 has no escalation and is also long Long waiting times between activities appear in 102 104 and 105 The investigation step seems to take a long time often overnight In 101 and 103 investigation to resolution is 1 hour and 30 minutes respectively

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential root causes Escalation process introduces delays In 102 and 105 after escalation there is a wait for Level 2 agent to pick up 2 point 5 hours and 28 hours The 28 hours in 105 is extreme

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Investigation step is a bottleneck In long cases investigation takes many hours 19 hours in 102 19 hours in 104 19 hours in 105 second investigation This suggests that investigation is complex or resources are limited maybe overnight no staff

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Long idle times before investigation In 104 3 point 5 hours between assign and investigate In 102 2 point 5 hours between assign and escalate then 2 point 5 hours to investigate In 105 only 10 min to first investigate but then long after escalation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible lack of resources during non business hours The long investigation times span overnight for example 14 00 to 09 00 next day If Level 2 agents are not available 24 by 7 tickets wait

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Reduce escalation handoff time Ensure Level 2 agents are available or have a faster handoff process

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Investigate why investigation takes so long Is it due to complexity If so maybe better triage to assign directly to Level 2 Or provide more training and tools to Level 1 to resolve without escalation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Monitor and reduce idle times Set service level targets for each step for example investigation must start within 1 hour of assignment

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Consider staffing for 24 by 7 coverage if tickets come in at all hours and require timely resolution

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the 105 case why was there an investigation before escalation Possibly Level 1 started investigation then escalated That might indicate that Level 1 attempted but could not resolve leading to rework Could improve triage to decide escalation earlier
